description: Self-reported: has a doctor/nurse ever told you that you had coronary heart disease (CHD) or a myocardial infarction (MI)?
Told by a health professional you have high blood pressure.
Told by a health professional you have high cholesterol.
Had a cholesterol check within the last 5 years.
Body Mass Index, self-reported height/weight.
Smoked at least 100 cigarettes in your entire life.
Ever told you had a stroke.
0=no diabetes, 1=prediabetes, 2=diabetes. The closest proxy to a glucose measurement available in this survey (BRFSS does not ask for a lab glucose value).
Physical activity in past 30 days, outside of a regular job.
Consumes fruit 1+ times per day.
Consumes vegetables 1+ times per day.
Heavy drinker (adult men >14 drinks/week, women >7 drinks/week).
Has any kind of health care coverage.
Needed to see a doctor in the past year but couldn't because of cost.
Self-rated general health: 1=excellent ... 5=poor.
Days of poor mental health in the past 30 days.
Days of poor physical health in the past 30 days.
Serious difficulty walking or climbing stairs.
Respondent sex.
13-level age category (1=18-24 ... 13=80+); not raw years.
Highest education level completed, 1 (none) to 6 (college grad).
Household income bracket, 1 (<$10k) to 8 (>=$75k).
